section_text_parts: This will give you editor support inside of your function, with error checks, completion, etc.
tutorial/path-params::section-0002::admo.section_text: This will give you editor support inside of your function, with error checks, completion, etc.

section_text_parts: Notice that the value your function received (and returned) is 3 , as a Python int , not a string "3" .
So, with that type declaration, FastAPI gives you automatic request "parsing" .
tutorial/path-params::section-0003::admo.section_text:
Notice that the value your function received (and returned) is 3 , as a Python int , not a string "3" .

So, with that type declaration, FastAPI gives you automatic request "parsing" .

section_text_parts: So, with the same Python type declaration, FastAPI gives you data validation.
Notice that the error also clearly states exactly the point where the validation didn't pass.
This is incredibly helpful while developing and debugging code that interacts with your API.
tutorial/path-params::section-0004::admo.section_text:
So, with the same Python type declaration, FastAPI gives you data validation.

Notice that the error also clearly states exactly the point where the validation didn't pass.

This is incredibly helpful while developing and debugging code that interacts with your API.

section_text_parts: Again, just with that same Python type declaration, FastAPI gives you automatic, interactive documentation (integrating Swagger UI).
Notice that the path parameter is declared to be an integer.
tutorial/path-params::section-0005::admo.section_text:
Again, just with that same Python type declaration, FastAPI gives you automatic, interactive documentation (integrating Swagger UI).

Notice that the path parameter is declared to be an integer.

section_text_parts: If you are wondering, "AlexNet", "ResNet", and "LeNet" are just names of Machine Learning models .
tutorial/path-params::section-0010::admo.section_text: If you are wondering, "AlexNet", "ResNet", and "LeNet" are just names of Machine Learning models .

section_text_parts: You could also access the value "lenet" with ModelName.lenet.value .
tutorial/path-params::section-0015::admo.section_text: You could also access the value "lenet" with ModelName.lenet.value .

section_text_parts: You might need the parameter to contain /home/johndoe/myfile.txt , with a leading slash ( / ).
In that case, the URL would be: /files//home/johndoe/myfile.txt , with a double slash ( // ) between files and home .

You might need the parameter to contain /home/johndoe/myfile.txt , with a leading slash ( / ).
